items.title: File Upload Vulnerability Leading to Privilege Escalation
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source (CVSS score: 9.6) could allow an attacker to achieve privilege escalation. Organizations are advised to install the latest updates to mitigate this risk.

items.title: Improper Encoding/Escaping Leading to Arbitrary Code Execution
items.summary: An improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.1) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution. This flaw requires immediate patching to prevent potential exploitation.

items.title: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source Security Update
items.summary: Adobe released a security update resolving critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation.

items.title: Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes
items.summary: A critical unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in the Amasty Order Attributes extension for Magento allows attackers to write executable files to a store without logging in. This vulnerability opens the door to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer (PolyShell)
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension. This flaw allows remote code execution via a crafted cookie and has been actively exploited in the wild.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento REST API
items.summary: Sansec identified PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that enables unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images. While a fix is in Magento 2.4.9, older supported versions remain vulnerable to this attack.

items.title: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source Security Update
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source addressing multiple vulnerabilities. These issues could lead to security feature bypass, application denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.
